behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is validation + row-index tracking + DLQ handoff across modules. A unit test of validator logic alone would miss bugs where the wrong row number is propagated or DLQ payload mapping is incorrect. Rejected System because behavior is semantic routing/metadata propagation and can be reproduced with in-process module wiring (stub input stream + in-memory DLQ sink), no required real OS/network/subprocess. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline components together on a small CSV fixture with missing required fields; assert invalid rows are emitted to DLQ with exact original row numbers and are excluded from normalized output.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: This is pure transformation logic at a single function/object boundary (date parser/normalizer). Real failure is parsing/normalization correctness, not module interaction. Rejected Integration because no required handoff/state boundary is needed to expose the failure; direct function tests with representative inputs catch it. Rejected higher levels since no OS/network/subprocess or AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven tests for the date-normalization function with ISO 8601, US, and European inputs (including ambiguous and invalid cases), asserting canonical output or explicit rejection.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode depends on real process memory behavior, runtime throughput, and timeout under load; in-process mocks can hide allocation patterns and scheduling effects. This crosses into OS/runtime characteristics, so System is the minimum. Rejected Integration because it would not faithfully surface OOM/timeout behavior at realistic scale.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the pipeline as a real process against a 500k-row fixture, with production-like timeout/memory limits; assert completion before timeout and peak memory stays below threshold (no OOM/kill).

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is between reader client, retry policy, and batch orchestration modules. A deterministic in-process fake S3 client returning 503 then success can expose incorrect retry/backoff logic and premature batch failure. Rejected System because this behavior does not require real network/S3 semantics to reproduce the specific failure mode; controlled module wiring is sufficient. Rejected Agentic/Workflow due no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Wire pipeline with a stub S3 client scripted to return 503 for first N attempts then succeed; use controllable clock/sleeper to assert exponential backoff intervals and verify batch eventually completes.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly includes mid-batch restart, which introduces process lifecycle/durability boundaries plus real DB persistence semantics. Real failures often appear only when the process is terminated/restarted and checkpoints/transactions recover against persisted state. Rejected Integration because re-instantiating components in one process can miss true restart durability issues. Rejected Agentic/Workflow since no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline against real PostgreSQL, force process stop mid-batch after partial commits, restart pipeline, and assert final table has exactly-once results (no duplicate keys/rows) for already written records.
